name: Elasticsearch API
what_it_does: Distributed search and analytics engine for full-text search, log analytics, and vector/hybrid search, available self-hosted or as Elastic Cloud with REST APIs for indexing and querying.
use_cases: Log and event analytics aggregation from agent-monitored systems
Full-text search over large document collections
Vector/hybrid search combining semantic and keyword search
Security event correlation and SIEM use cases
Complex aggregation queries for business intelligence
not_for: Simple hosted search without infrastructure management experience
best_when: You need powerful full-text search AND analytics/aggregations at scale, especially for log analysis or complex search use cases.
avoid_when: You want a simple managed search solution - Elasticsearch has significant operational complexity.

auth.notes: API keys with fine-grained index-level privileges. Basic auth available but not recommended. Bearer token in Authorization header. Elastic Cloud adds additional IAM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: free and open source (Elastic License 2.0); Elastic Cloud: 14-day trial
pricing.paid_tiers: Elastic Cloud from $95/month for 1GB RAM; self-hosted infrastructure costs vary
pricing.notes: Self-hosted is free but requires infrastructure. Elastic Cloud pricing by compute and storage. Elastic License 2.0 restricts SaaS reselling.

performance.rate_limits: No inherent rate limits; depends on cluster capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Detailed error responses with reason, type, and root_cause. Stack traces in debug mode helpful for agents troubleshooting query syntax.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document indexing with explicit ID is idempotent. Bulk API supports upsert semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: Query DSL is complex - agents frequently construct malformed queries
Mapping must be configured before indexing or auto-mapping may create wrong field types
Shard count is set at index creation and cannot be changed without reindex
MCP server is community-maintained by Elastic but not the primary supported interface
Version conflicts on concurrent updates require retry with optimistic locking
